title: The development of the London underground railway

sentences.en: In the first half of the 1800s, London’s population grew at an astonishing rate, and the central area became increasingly congested.
sentences.zh: 19世纪上半叶，伦敦人口以惊人的速度增长，中心城区也变得越来越拥堵。

grammar.type: 并列句 + 比较级递进
grammar.note: 主干是 population grew 与 area became congested；at an astonishing rate 作方式状语；increasingly 强调拥堵程度逐步加深。

sentences.en: In addition, the expansion of the overground railway network resulted in more and more passengers arriving in the capital.
sentences.zh: 此外，地上铁路网络的扩张导致越来越多的乘客抵达首都。

grammar.note: the expansion 是名词化主语；resulted in 表“导致”；arriving in the capital 为现在分词短语，修饰 passengers。

sentences.en: However, in 1846, a Royal Commission decided that the railways should not be allowed to enter the City, the capital’s historic and business centre.
sentences.zh: 然而，1846年，一个皇家委员会决定不应允许铁路进入伦敦城——这个首都的历史与商业中心。

grammar.note: decided 后接 that 宾语从句；should not be allowed 是被动；逗号后的名词短语解释 the City。

sentences.en: The result was that the overground railway stations formed a ring around the City.
sentences.zh: 结果是，地上铁路车站在伦敦城周围形成了一个环。

grammar.note: The result was that... 用来引出前文决定造成的结果；formed a ring around 表空间布局。

sentences.en: The area within consisted of poorly built, overcrowded slums and the streets were full of horse-drawn traffic.
sentences.zh: 环内区域由建筑质量很差且拥挤不堪的贫民窟组成，街道上满是马车交通。

grammar.note: consisted of 表“由……组成”；poorly built 与 overcrowded 并列修饰 slums；and 连接另一分句。

sentences.en: Crossing the City became a nightmare.
sentences.zh: 穿过伦敦城成了一场噩梦。

grammar.note: Crossing the City 是动名词短语作主语；became a nightmare 概括交通困难。

sentences.en: It could take an hour and a half to travel 8 km by horse-drawn carriage or bus.
sentences.zh: 乘马车或公共马车走 8 公里可能要花一个半小时。

grammar.note: It could take...to travel... 是花费时间句型；by horse-drawn carriage or bus 表交通方式。

sentences.en: Numerous schemes were proposed to resolve these problems, but few succeeded.
sentences.zh: 人们提出了许多方案来解决这些问题，但很少有方案成功。

grammar.note: schemes were proposed 是被动；to resolve... 表目的；but few succeeded 形成转折。

sentences.en: Amongst the most vocal advocates for a solution to London’s traffic problems was Charles Pearson, who worked as a solicitor for the City of London.
sentences.zh: 在为解决伦敦交通问题而大声疾呼的人中，查尔斯·皮尔逊是最积极的一位；他是伦敦城的一名律师。

grammar.type: 倒装结构 + 非限制性定语从句
grammar.note: Amongst...was Charles Pearson 为地点/范围状语提前引起的倒装；who worked... 补充人物身份。

sentences.en: He saw both social and economic advantages in building an underground railway that would link the overground railway stations together and clear London slums at the same time.
sentences.zh: 他看到，修建一条把地上铁路车站连接起来、同时清理伦敦贫民窟的地下铁路，既有社会利益，也有经济利益。

grammar.type: see advantages in doing + 定语从句
grammar.note: in building... 作介词宾语；that would link...and clear... 修饰 railway，说明铁路功能。

sentences.en: His idea was to relocate the poor workers who lived in the inner-city slums to newly constructed suburbs, and to provide cheap rail travel for them to get to work.
sentences.zh: 他的想法是把住在内城贫民窟的贫困工人迁到新建郊区，并为他们提供廉价铁路交通去上班。

grammar.type: 不定式作表语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: was to relocate...and to provide... 是并列不定式表方案内容；who lived... 修饰 workers。

sentences.en: Pearson’s ideas gained support amongst some businessmen and in 1851 he submitted a plan to Parliament.
sentences.zh: 皮尔逊的想法得到了一些商人的支持，并且在1851年他向议会提交了一份计划。

grammar.note: gained support 与 submitted a plan 并列；amongst some businessmen 对应题目中的 A number of businessmen。

sentences.en: It was rejected, but coincided with a proposal from another group for an underground connecting line, which Parliament passed.
sentences.zh: 该计划被否决了，但它恰好与另一个团体提出的地下连接线方案同时出现，而这个方案获得了议会通过。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + which 定语从句
grammar.note: It was rejected 是被动；coincided with 表“与……同时发生”；which Parliament passed 修饰 proposal。

sentences.en: The two groups merged and established the Metropolitan Railway Company in August 1854.
sentences.zh: 这两个团体合并，并于1854年8月成立了大都会铁路公司。

grammar.note: merged 与 established 并列，交代组织形成。

sentences.en: The company’s plan was to construct an underground railway line from the Great Western Railway’s station at Paddington to the edge of the City at Farringdon Street - a distance of almost 5 km.
sentences.zh: 公司的计划是修建一条地下铁路线，从帕丁顿的大西部铁路车站通往伦敦城边缘的法灵顿街，全程将近5公里。

grammar.type: 不定式作表语 + 插入解释
grammar.note: was to construct... 表计划内容；from...to... 说明路线；破折号后补充距离。

sentences.en: The organisation had difficulty in raising the funding for such a radical and expensive scheme, not least because of the critical articles printed by the press.
sentences.zh: 该组织很难为这样一个激进而昂贵的方案筹集资金，尤其是因为报刊刊登了批评文章。

grammar.type: have difficulty in doing + 原因状语
grammar.note: had difficulty in raising... 是固定结构；not least because... 强调重要原因，对应 funding 与 press 两题。

sentences.en: Objectors argued that the tunnels would collapse under the weight of traffic overhead, buildings would be shaken and passengers would be poisoned by the emissions from the train engines.
sentences.zh: 反对者声称，隧道会在上方交通重量下坍塌，建筑会被震动，乘客会被火车发动机排放的废气毒害。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + 并列被动
grammar.note: argued 后接三个并列 that 内容；collapse / be shaken / be poisoned 构成反对理由。

sentences.en: However, Pearson and his partners persisted.
sentences.zh: 然而，皮尔逊和他的伙伴们坚持了下来。

grammar.note: However 承接反对声音后转折；persisted 强调持续推进。

sentences.en: The GWR, aware that the new line would finally enable them to run trains into the heart of the City, invested almost £250,000 in the scheme.
sentences.zh: 大西部铁路公司意识到新线路最终会让他们把火车开进伦敦城中心，于是向该方案投资了近25万英镑。

grammar.type: 分词插入语 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: aware that... 是形容词短语作插入状语；invested...in... 表投资。

sentences.en: Eventually, over a five-year period, £1m was raised.
sentences.zh: 最终，在五年时间里筹集到了100万英镑。

grammar.note: was raised 是被动，强调资金被筹集完成；over a five-year period 表时间跨度。

sentences.en: The chosen route ran beneath existing main roads to minimise the expense of demolishing buildings.
sentences.zh: 所选路线位于现有主干道下方，以尽量减少拆除建筑物的费用。

grammar.note: ran beneath... 表路线位置；to minimise... 是目的状语。

sentences.en: Originally scheduled to be completed in 21 months, the construction of the underground line took three years.
sentences.zh: 地下线路原定21个月完工，但施工实际用了三年。

grammar.type: 过去分词状语 + 对比
grammar.note: Originally scheduled... 为过去分词短语，表示原计划；took three years 与 21 months 对比。

sentences.en: It was built just below street level using a technique known as ‘cut and cover’.
sentences.zh: 它建在刚低于街面的地方，采用一种被称为“明挖回填法”的技术。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + 过去分词定语
grammar.note: was built 是被动；known as ‘cut and cover’ 修饰 technique。

sentences.en: A trench about ten metres wide and six metres deep was dug, and the sides temporarily held up with timber beams.
sentences.zh: 人们挖出一条约十米宽、六米深的沟渠，并用木梁临时支撑两侧。

grammar.note: was dug 与 were held up 并列被动；about...wide/deep 描述尺寸。

sentences.en: Brick walls were then constructed, and finally a brick arch was added to create a tunnel.
sentences.zh: 随后建起砖墙，最后再加上砖拱，形成隧道。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + 目的状语
grammar.note: were constructed 与 was added 并列；to create a tunnel 表结果/目的。

sentences.en: A two-metre-deep layer of soil was laid on top of the tunnel and the road above rebuilt.
sentences.zh: 隧道顶部铺上一层两米厚的土，随后上方道路被重建。

grammar.note: was laid 与 rebuilt 省略并列助动词，说明明挖回填的最后步骤；soil 对应第6题。

sentences.en: The Metropolitan line, which opened on 10 January 1863, was the world’s first underground railway.
sentences.zh: 1863年1月10日开通的大都会线，是世界上第一条地下铁路。

grammar.note: which opened... 补充开通时间；the world’s first underground railway 是关键信息，判断第7题 FALSE。

sentences.en: On its first day, almost 40,000 passengers were carried between Paddington and Farringdon, the journey taking about 18 minutes.
sentences.zh: 开通第一天，帕丁顿和法灵顿之间运送了近4万名乘客，全程约18分钟。

grammar.note: passengers were carried 是被动；the journey taking... 为独立主格补充行程耗时。

sentences.en: By the end of the Metropolitan’s first year of operation, 9.5 million journeys had been made.
sentences.zh: 到大都会线运营第一年结束时，已经完成了950万次出行。

grammar.note: had been made 表示到过去某个时间点已经完成的出行次数。

sentences.en: Even as the Metropolitan began operation, the first extensions to the line were being authorised; these were built over the next five years, reaching Moorgate in the east of London and Hammersmith in the west.
sentences.zh: 甚至在大都会线刚开始运营时，第一批延长线就已获批；这些延长线在接下来五年建成，东到伦敦的穆尔盖特，西到哈默史密斯。

grammar.type: 时间从句 + 分号并列
grammar.note: Even as 引导时间背景；were being authorised 为过去进行时被动；reaching... 补充延伸方向。

sentences.en: The original plan was to pull the trains with steam locomotives, using firebricks in the boilers to provide steam, but these engines were never introduced.
sentences.zh: 最初的计划是用蒸汽机车牵引列车，并在锅炉中使用耐火砖来产生蒸汽，但这些发动机从未投入使用。

grammar.type: 不定式作表语 + 转折
grammar.note: was to pull... 表计划；using... 作方式；but 转折说明未采用。

sentences.en: Instead, the line used specially designed locomotives that were fitted with water tanks in which steam could be condensed.
sentences.zh: 相反，该线路使用特别设计的机车，这些机车装有水箱，可使蒸汽冷凝。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 被动语态
grammar.note: that were fitted with... 修饰 locomotives；in which steam could be condensed 继续说明 water tanks 功能。

sentences.en: However, smoke and fumes remained a problem, even though ventilation shafts were added to the tunnels.
sentences.zh: 然而，尽管隧道增加了通风井，烟雾和废气仍然是个问题。

grammar.note: even though 引导让步；remained a problem 对应第9题 TRUE。

sentences.en: Despite the extension of the underground railway, by the 1880s, congestion on London’s streets had become worse.
sentences.zh: 尽管地下铁路不断延伸，到19世纪80年代，伦敦街道的拥堵却变得更加严重。

grammar.note: Despite 后接名词短语；by the 1880s 表到某时为止；had become worse 表变化结果。

sentences.en: The problem was partly that the existing underground lines formed a circuit around the centre of London and extended to the suburbs, but did not cross the capital’s centre.
sentences.zh: 问题部分在于，现有地下线路围绕伦敦中心形成了一个环并延伸到郊区，却没有穿过首都中心。

grammar.type: 表语从句 + but 转折
grammar.note: The problem was partly that... 解释问题原因；formed / extended / did not cross 形成对比。

sentences.en: The ‘cut and cover’ method of construction was not an option in this part of the capital.
sentences.zh: 在首都的这一部分，“明挖回填”施工法并不可行。

grammar.note: was not an option 表“不是可选方案、不可行”，对应第10题需要 different approach。

sentences.en: The only alternative was to tunnel deep underground.
sentences.zh: 唯一的替代方案是在地下深处开凿隧道。

grammar.note: The only alternative was to... 表唯一解决路径；deep underground 强调深层隧道。

sentences.en: Although the technology to create these tunnels existed, steam locomotives could not be used in such a confined space.
sentences.zh: 尽管建造这些隧道的技术已经存在，蒸汽机车却不能在如此狭小的空间内使用。

grammar.note: Although 引出已有条件；could not be used 是被动，说明限制。

sentences.en: It wasn’t until the development of a reliable electric motor, and a means of transferring power from the generator to a moving train, that the world’s first deep-level electric railway, the City & South London, became possible.
sentences.zh: 直到可靠电动机以及把发电机动力传输到移动列车的方法发展出来，世界第一条深层电气铁路——城市与南伦敦铁路——才成为可能。

grammar.note: 强调结构突出技术突破是前提；a means of transferring power 与 reliable electric motor 并列。

sentences.en: The line opened in 1890, and ran from the City to Stockwell, south of the River Thames.
sentences.zh: 该线路于1890年开通，从伦敦城通往泰晤士河南岸的斯托克韦尔。

grammar.note: opened 与 ran 并列；south of the River Thames 补充 Stockwell 的位置。

sentences.en: The trains were made up of three carriages and driven by electric engines.
sentences.zh: 列车由三节车厢组成，并由电力发动机驱动。

grammar.note: were made up of 表“由……组成”；driven by 表动力来源。

sentences.en: The carriages were narrow and had tiny windows just below the roof because it was thought that passengers would not want to look out at the tunnel walls.
sentences.zh: 车厢很窄，窗户很小且位于靠近车顶的位置，因为当时认为乘客不会想看隧道墙壁。

grammar.note: had tiny windows just below the roof 说明窗户位置，不是 eye level；because 解释设计原因。

sentences.en: The line was not without its problems, mainly caused by an unreliable power supply.
sentences.zh: 这条线路并非没有问题，问题主要由不可靠的供电造成。

grammar.type: 双重否定 + 过去分词短语
grammar.note: was not without its problems 表“确实有问题”；mainly caused by... 说明问题来源。

sentences.en: Although the City & South London Railway was a great technical achievement, it did not make a profit.
sentences.zh: 虽然城市与南伦敦铁路是一项伟大的技术成就，但它没有盈利。

grammar.note: Although 引出让步；did not make a profit 对应第12题 FALSE。

sentences.en: Then, in 1900, the Central London Railway, known as the ‘Tuppenny Tube’, began operation using new electric locomotives.
sentences.zh: 随后，1900年，被称为“两便士地铁”的中央伦敦铁路开始运营，使用新的电力机车。

grammar.type: 过去分词插入 + 分词状语
grammar.note: known as... 补充 Central London Railway 的绰号；using... 表运营方式。

sentences.en: It was very popular and soon afterwards new railways and extensions were added to the growing tube network.
sentences.zh: 它非常受欢迎，不久之后，新的铁路和延长线被加入不断扩大的地铁网络。

grammar.note: was very popular 与 were added 并列；growing tube network 表逐步扩展的系统。

sentences.en: By 1907, the heart of today’s Underground system was in place.
sentences.zh: 到1907年，今天伦敦地铁系统的核心已经形成。

grammar.note: was in place 表“已经就位、形成”；heart 指系统核心。

questions.title: Questions 1-6 · Note completion
instructions: Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

items.prompt: The ___ of London increased rapidly between 1800 and 1850.
Building the railway would make it possible to move people to better housing in the ___.
A number of ___ agreed with Pearson’s idea.
The company initially had problems getting the ___ needed for the project.
Negative articles about the project appeared in the ___.
With the completion of the brick arch, the tunnel was covered with ___.

questions.title: Questions 7-13 · TRUE / FALSE / NOT GIVEN
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?

items.prompt: Other countries had built underground railways before the Metropolitan line opened.
More people than predicted travelled on the Metropolitan line on the first day.
The use of ventilation shafts failed to prevent pollution in the tunnels.
A different approach from the ‘cut and cover’ technique was required in London’s central area.
The windows on City & South London trains were at eye level.
The City & South London Railway was a financial success.
Trains on the ‘Tuppenny Tube’ nearly always ran on time.

phrases.def: 伦敦金融城；伦敦老城商业中心
两便士地铁；中央伦敦铁路绰号
